mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline months.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 65% of triggered months, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, which increases corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses predictive power in multivariate panel regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of recovery-phase observations, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 70% of high-inflation months, or the threshold signal shows no predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in panel regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter increases in real gross domestic product reflect expanding economic output and corporate profit margins, which directly translate to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDP growth signal yields negative forward returns in more than 60% of quarters, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals a severe supply-side contraction and impending recession, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 65% of triggered months, or the threshold signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in out-of-sample testing.
